Enable Subrequest Shipping

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify if sub-requests to source databases are
                       executed separately as standalone queries or executed together.
                       By default, sub-requests are shipped separately which can improve performance if you
                       execute complex reports with a large group of sub-requests, that is, you prefer to ship the sub-
                       requests separately in multiple simplified queries rather than ship a large single complicated
                       query all at once.
                       In Oracle BI Enterprise Edition, the default is set to NO. If you used Oracle BI Enterprise Edition
                       and want to retain the previous default behavior, set this property to NO to continue executing
                       database sub-requests together.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ Default -- Database sub-requests are shipped separately. This is the same as the value

                             YES. Default
                       ｷ YES -- Database sub-requests are shipped separately.
                       ｷ NO -- Database sub-requests are shipped together, all at once.

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- EnableSubrequestShipping

                       API Values --
                       ｷ Default
                       ｷ YES
                       ｷ NO

                       Documentation Links
                       ｷ When Do System Settings Take Effect?
                       ｷ Typical Workflow for Using System Setting REST APIs
                       ｷ Examples for System Setting REST APIs

Enforce Safe Domains in Actions

                       Administrators use this system setting to determine whether action links that users add to
                       analyses and dashboards can invoke any URL or only URLs that administrators specify in the
                       safe domains list.
                       This system setting applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data
                       visualizations or pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Don't allow actions to invoke any URL that's not in the safe domain list. Default for

                             a brand new service
                       ｷ Off -- Allow actions to invoke any URL, even if the URL isn't listed as a safe domain.

                             Default for an existing service

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Enterprise only.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- EnforceSafeDomainsActions

                       API Values --
                       ｷ true
                       ｷ false

                       Documentation Links
                       ｷ When Do System Settings Take Effect?
                       ｷ Typical Workflow for Using System Setting REST APIs
                       ｷ Examples for System Setting REST APIs

Hide EPM Cloud Members with No Access

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify if users can view all EPM dimension members
                       in a hierarchy prompt list of values or when adding the hierarchy to a canvas, even if they don't
                       have data access to some of the members.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Show only those members of an EPM dimension that users have data access to. If

                             this setting is On, users who don't have access to the root member of the dimension
                             hierarchy won't see any EPM members in hierarchies or hierarchy prompts.

Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                            Chapter 8
                                                                                                                              Other System Settings

                       ｷ Off -- Users can view all the members in an EPM dimension even if they don't have
                             access to view data for some members. Default

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       ｷ true
                       ｷ false

                       analyses, dashboards, or pixel-perfect reports.

                       ｷ Off -- Detailed loading messages are displayed. Default

                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

        ｷ Examples for System Setting REST APIs

        Administrators use this system setting to apply a specific locale setting to content migrated
        from Oracle BI Enterprise Edition.
        After you migrate content from your Oracle BI Enterprise Edition environment to Oracle
        Analytics, you may see a different language in messages, dates, or currencies within analyses.
        For example, if you look at a migrated analysis in Polish, the currencies or dates might display
        based on the Oracle Analytics default locale, not the original Oracle BI Enterprise Edition
        locale. To preserve the Oracle BI Enterprise Edition currencies and dates in Oracle Analytics,
        change this setting to Polish.
        This system setting applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data
        visualizations or pixel-perfect reports.

        Valid Values
        ｷ Arabic
        ｷ Chinese
        ｷ Chinese-traditional
        ｷ Croatian
        ｷ Czech
        ｷ Danish
        ｷ Dutch
        ｷ English-USA
        ｷ Finnish
        ｷ French
        ｷ German
        ｷ Greek
        ｷ Hebrew
        ｷ Hungarian
        ｷ Italian

Portal Path

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify the path of the dashboard page that's
                       displayed by default when users sign in to Oracle Analytics. For example, /shared/ <folder>/
                       _portal/<name>.
                       You can specify a single path for all users and multiple paths by user role, for example
                       {"application role 1":"catalog dashboard path 1","application role 2":"catalog
                       dashboard path 2","default":"catalog dashboard path 3"}.
                       This setting applies to all users, but users can override it after they've signed in.
                       You can enter a maximum of 5,000 characters in this field.
                       This system setting applies only to the Classic home page.

                       Valid Values
                       Valid path.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

Recursive Datetime Type Checking

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether to enforce strict recursive data type
                       checking for comparisons between identical data types (for example, integer to integer) or non-
                       compatible data types (for example, integer to short integer) on all data sources or with all
                       datasets.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Enforces strict recursive checking for identical or noncompatible data types on all

                             data sources or datasets. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Relaxes strict recursive checking for date and time data types on all data sources or

                             datasets. However, if there are too many data type inconsistencies, you may want to
                             change the data types to be compatible or use constants of the correct data type when
                             comparing a column to a value. For example, after you migrate content from Oracle BI
                             Enterprise Edition to Oracle Analytics, you might start seeing this type of check error in
                             your reports because eary versions of Oracle BI Enterprise Edition didn't enforce strict
                             checks:
                             [nQSError: 22024] A comparison is being carried out between non-compatible
                             types <type1> and <type2>

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- RecursiveDatetimeTypeChecking

                       API Values --
                       ｷ YES

                                                                                                                              Other System Settings

                       ｷ NO

Repeat Rows on Excel Exports for Tables and Pivots

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether cells that span rows and cells that
                       span columns are repeated when exporting tables and pivot tables to Excel.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- If switched on, cells that span rows and cells that span columns are repeated,

                             regardless of the Value Suppression setting in the Analysis editor.
                       ｷ Off -- If switched off, the Value Suppression setting in the Analysis editor is honored and

                             cells that span rows and cells that span columns don't repeat when exporting tables and
                             pivot tables to Excel. Default

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Enterprise only.

                       ｷ true
                       ｷ false

Sort Null Values First

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether to sort NULL values before other
                       values (On) or after (Off). Select the value that matches your database. If this setting doesn't
                       match your database setting, then the database setting takes precedence.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Sorts NULL values before other values.
                       ｷ Off -- Sorts NULL values after other values. Default

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       ｷ YES
                       ｷ NO

Sort Order Locale

                       Administrators use this system setting to apply a locale setting to determine how content
                       migrated from Oracle BI Enterprise Edition is sorted.
                       After you migrate content from your Oracle BI Enterprise Edition environment to Oracle
                       Analytics, you may experience different sorting behaviors in analyses.
                       For example, if you look at a migrated analysis in Polish, the upper case and lower case letters
                       might sort based on the Oracle Analytics default locale, not the original Oracle BI Enterprise
                       Edition locale. To preserve the Oracle BI Enterprise Edition sort behavior in Oracle Analytics,
                       change this setting to Polish.
                       This system setting applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data
                       visualizations or pixel-perfect reports.

Always Use DBMS_RANDOM in Oracle Data Sources

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether Contextual Insight algorithms use the
                       DBMS_RANDOM package in Oracle data sources to generate random numbers.

                       This system setting applies only to data visualizations. It doesn't apply to analyses,
                       dashboards, or pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values

                       ｷ On -- Contextual Insights always use the DBMS_RANDOM package in Oracle data sources for
                             random number generation. Default

                       ｷ Off -- Contextual Insights uses the value of the RAND_SUPPORTED property to
                             determine whether or not to use the DBMS_RANDOM database feature. Semantic modelers
                             can set the RAND_SUPPORTED property to ON or OFF in the query features selection
                             pane for their Oracle data source. See Modify a Database's Data Source Properties and
                             Supported Query Features. If RAND_SUPPORTED is OFF, the ORA_HASH function is used.

                       Apply Required

                       Yes.

                       Change Effective

                       After a few minutes.

Default Limit Values By for Filter

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify the default behavior of the workbook filter
                       option Limit Values By.
                       Users can override the default that you select here, within their workbooks.
                       This system setting applies only to data visualizations and workbooks. It doesn't apply to
                       analyses, dashboards, or pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ Auto -- By default, workbook filters use the Auto behavior, that is, allow other filters in the

                             workbook (if any) to limit values. This setting can improve user experience. Default
                       ｷ None -- By default, workbook filters aren't limited by any other filter (if any). This setting

                             can improve performance.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       Immediately.

                       Edition
                       Enterprise only.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- DefaultLimitValuesByForFilters

                       API Values (UI Label) --
                       ｷ auto (Auto)
                       ｷ none (None)

Enable Database Analytics Node in Data Flows

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether the Database Analytics node is
                       displayed in data flows.
                       This system setting applies only to data flows. It doesn't apply to analyses, dashboards, or
                       pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- The Database Analytics node is available in data flows so that data flow designers

                             can apply database analytics functions to the data. Default
                       ｷ Off -- The Database Analytics node isn't available in data flows. This prevents data flow

                             designers from generating a potentially high number of SQL statements and slowing
                             database performance.

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       After a few minutes.

Enable Enhancements to Limit Values By in Workbooks

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether the Limit Values By workbook filter
                       option is determined by the location of the filter within the workbook or the filter scope.

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                     April 15, 2026

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Enhanced filters behavior. Limits a filter's values by all filters in higher locations or

                             all filters in the same location when they're higher on the drill path or in an unrelated drill
                             path. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Old filters behavior. Limits a filter's values by any other filters that are higher on the
                             drill path or in an unrelated drill path, unless the filter being limited has a higher filter scope.

Enable Lazy Load for Workbook Canvases

                       Administrators use this system setting to load column information for only the first canvas when
                       a user opens a workbook, so the workbook loads faster. Additional canvases load column
                       information when the user views them.
                       Column information includes column name, data type, column formula, column heading, and
                       so on.
                       This system setting applies only to data visualizations and workbooks. It doesn't apply to
                       analyses, dashboards, or pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Loads column information for only the first canvas when a user opens a workbook.

                             Information for other canvases loads when the user views them. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Loads column information for all workbook canvases when a user opens a

                             workbook.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After browser refresh.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values

Evaluate Support Level

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify who can issue database functions: EVALUATE,
                       EVALUATE_ANALYTIC, EVALUATE_AGGR, and EVALUATE_PREDICATE. By default (0), the EVALUATE
                       database functions are disabled.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ 0 (or any other value)-- No one. All EVALUATE database functions are disabled in Oracle

                             Analytics. Default
                       ｷ 1 -- Service administrators only. Users with the BI Service Administrator application role

                             can invoke EVALUATE database functions.
                       ｷ 2 -- Anyone. Any user who signs in to Oracle Analytics can invoke EVALUATE database

                             functions.

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- EvaluateSupportLevel

                       API Values --
                       ｷ0
                       ｷ1
                       ｷ2

                       Documentation Links
                       ｷ Evaluate Functions
                       ｷ When Do System Settings Take Effect?

Maximum Working File Percent Size

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify that the temporary file doesn't exceed a
                       specified percentage of the global work directory size limit.
                       The size limit defaults for temporary files is 5% (of 100 GB), equivalent to 5 GB. The file limit
                       applies individually to each temporary file, while the size specified for the total global work
                       directory applies collectively to all temporary files created.
                       You can increase or decrease this value within the range of 5% to 50%. This enables
                       temporary file sizes between 5GB and 50GB. Raising this setting above 50% limits
                       concurrency for large operations.

                       Valid Values
                       5-50
                       Default value is 5

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Enterprise only.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- MaximumWorkingFilePercentSize

                       API Values -- <Number between 5 and 50>

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                     April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Page 50 of 99

                       ｷ Typical Workflow for Using System Setting REST APIs
                       ｷ Examples for System Setting REST APIs

OBIEE Compatibility Release

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify the on-premise Oracle BI Enterprise Edition
                       version number for feature compatibility. This only applies if you upgrade from Oracle BI
                       Enterprise Edition to Oracle Analytics, and you want to use a feature from a specific on-
                       premises release in Oracle Analytics.

                       Valid Values

                       depend on the size of your Oracle Analytics service.

                       Valid Values

                       Maximum, 50% of Maximum, 40% of Maximum, 30% of Maximum, 20% of Maximum, 10% of
                       Maximum, Minimum - 1000 rows
                       Default is Maximum - no restriction

Strong Datetime Type Checking

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether to enforce strict checking for date
                       and time data types and whether to reject queries that contain incompatibilities in date and
                       time data types.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Enforces strict checking for date and time data types. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Relaxes strict checking for date and time data types. However, invalid queries or

                             queries with severe date and time incompatibilities may still be still rejected. For example,
                             date and time incompatibilities might be rejected if your relational database uses strict
                             checking for those data types.

                       Apply Required
                       Yes.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition

Use Content Delivery Network (CDN) to Load Resources

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether Oracle Analytics can use Oracle's
                       content delivery network (CDN) to load the resources used by Oracle Analytics faster.
                       Without a CDN, resources are served directly from Oracle Analytics. This can result in higher
                       latency and slower load times compared to using a distributed CDN, especially for users who
                       are physically far from the hosting server.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Allow Oracle Analytics to use a CDN to load resources. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Don't use a CDN. Load resources directly from the origin server.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- EnableUseCdn

                       API Values --
                       ｷ YES
                       ｷ NO

                       Documentation Links
                       ｷ When Do System Settings Take Effect?
                       ｷ Typical Workflow for Using System Setting REST APIs
                       ｷ Examples for System Setting REST APIs

Preview System Settings

                       Administrators use these system settings to turn some preview features on and off. The
                       administrator turns a preview feature on so that your organization can evaluate and learn how
                       to use new features before they are available by default in Oracle Analytics.

                       System Settings:
                       ｷ Enable Advanced Natural Language Query Search Results (Preview)
                       ｷ Enable Details Pane on the Home Page (Preview)
                       ｷ Enable Geometry Data Type (Preview)
                       ｷ Enable Shared Filter Groups in Workbooks (Preview)
                       ｷ Enable Time Zones in Workbooks (Preview)

                       search is available to users.
                       The advanced natural language query search transforms natural language query results into
                       visualizations.
                       This system setting doesn't apply to the Classic home page.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Enables the advanced natural language query search. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Reverts to the standard search behavior.

Prompt System Settings

                       Administrators use these options to configure prompt behavior in analyses and dashboards.
                       For example, enable search results to automatically display as highlighted when users enter
                       search parameters, without the need to click Search.

                       System Settings:
                       ｷ Auto Apply Dashboard Prompt Values
                       ｷ Auto Search on Prompt Value Search Dialog
                       ｷ Case Insensitive Auto Complete
                       ｷ Show Null Value When Column Is Nullable
                       ｷ Support Auto Complete

Auto Apply Dashboard Prompt Values

                       Administrators use this system setting to enable the option to hide the Apply button so that
                       prompt values can be applied without clicking any button.
                       This option applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data visualizations or
                       pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Displays the following fields and options. Default

                             ｭ Displays the Show Apply Button and Show Reset Button fields in the Edit Page
                                  Settings dialog.

                             ｭ Displays the Prompts Apply Buttons and Prompts Reset Buttons fields in the
                                  Dashboard Properties dialog.

                             ｭ Displays the Prompt Buttons on Current Page option on the dashboard builder's
                                  Tools menu.

                       ｷ Off -- Hides the fields and options mentioned above.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

Case Insensitive Auto Complete

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether, when a user enters a prompt value
                       in analyses and dashboards, the auto-complete functionality is case-insensitive.
                       This option applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data visualizations or
                       pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Case isn't considered when a user enters a prompt value such as "Oracle" or

                             "oracle." Default
                       ｷ Off -- Case is considered when a user enters a prompt value, so the user must enter

                             "Oracle" and not "oracle" to find the Oracle record.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Enterprise only.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- AutoCompletePromptDropDownsCaseInsensitive

                       API Values --
                       ｷ true
                       ｷ false

Show Null Value When Column Is Nullable

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether to show the term "NULL" at runtime
                       in the column prompt above the column separator in the drop-down list when the database
                       allows null values.
                       This option applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data visualizations and
                       pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ always -- Always shows the term "NULL" above the column separator in the drop-down

                             list. Default
                       ｷ never -- Never shows the term "NULL" in the drop-down list.
                       ｷ asDataValue -- Displays the data value in the drop-down list, not the term "NULL" above

                             the separator in the drop-down list.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Enterprise only.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- AnalysisPromptsShowNullValueWhenColumnIsNullable

                       API Values --
                       ｷ always
                       ｷ never
                       ｷ asDataValue

                                                       Security System Settings

Allow HTML/JavaScript/CSS Content

                       Administrators use this system setting to determine whether users can apply and save HTML,
                       JavaScript, and CSS markup in various text fields for analyses and dashboards, and how any
                       previously saved markup is used.

                       This option applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data visualizations or
                       pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values

                       ｷ Always -- Enables users to apply markup. Displays the Contains HTML/JavaScript/CSS
                             Markup option in dialogs where additional formatting might be useful. For example:
                             ｭ For analyses: Various dialogs in the analysis editor, Analysis Properties dialog, Column
                                  Properties (Column Format) dialog, Edit Column Formula dialog, Narrative dialog,
                                  Ticker dialog, Static Text dialog, and New Calculated Measure dialog.
                             ｭ For dashboards: Various dialogs in the dashboard editor, Text Properties dialog, and
                                  Edit Header and Edit Footer dialogs (under Print and Export Options).

                       ｷ Never -- Prevents users from applying markup. Hides the Contains HTML/
                             JavaScript/CSS Markup option. Users can only enter plain text. Oracle Analytics ignores
                             any markup that users previously entered and saved for their analyses and dashboards.

                       ｷ HTML Only -- Enables users to apply HTML markup. Displays the Contains HTML/
                             JavaScript/CSS Markup option in dialogs where additional formatting might be useful but
                             only safe HTML is allowed (no JavaScript or CSS). When an analysis or dashboard opens,
                             Oracle Analytics sanitizes any markup that users have entered and applies only the HTML
                             markup.

                       ｷ On Open -- Prevents users from applying additional markup (existing markup is retained).
                             Hides the Contains HTML/JavaScript/CSS Markup option so users can enter only plain
                             text. Any previously saved markup for analyses and dashboards continues to be applied.
                             Default

                       Apply Required

                       No.

                       Change Effective

                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition

                       Enterprise only.

                       API Key and Values

                       API Key -- AllowHTMLJavaScriptCSSContent

                       API Values (UI Label) --
                       ｷ never (Never)
                       ｷ sanitized (HTML Only)
                       ｷ false (On Open)

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                     April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Page 65 of 99
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.

                                                                                                                           Security System Settings

                       ｷ true (Always)

                       Documentation Links

Save Workbook Thumbnail

                       Administrators use this system setting to help people identify workbook content, Oracle
                       Analytics can display thumbnail images for workbooks on the home page. The information
                       that's shown in these thumbnails is blurred to protect sensitive data from being exposed to
                       users that don't have the same access as data authors.
                       This setting overrides any Save thumbnails value set in the Workbook Properties dialog at the
                       individual workbook level.
                       This setting doesn't apply to watchlists because they don't use thumbnails. Instead, watchlists
                       display miniaturized visualizations that reload whenever you refresh the home page.
                       This system setting doesn't apply to the Classic home page.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Display blurred workbook thumbnails on the home page. If this setting enabled (on),

                             workbook owners can hide the thumbnail for individual workbooks if they need to. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Don't display any workbook thumbnails on the home page. Instead, show the

                             standard icon for all workbooks.

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- SaveWorkbookThumbnail

Time System Settings

                       Administrators use these options to configure default time zone settings.

                       System Settings:
                       ｷ Apply Default User Time Zone for Workbooks
                       ｷ Data Offset for Analyses and Dashboards
                       ｷ Default User Time Zone
                       ｷ Time Zone for Analyses, Dashboard, Workbooks

Apply Default User Time Zone for Workbooks

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether the Default User Time Zone system
                       setting is applied to the data in workbooks.
                       For workbooks, users can specify a time zone in the My Profile dialog to override the applied
                       Default User Time Zone system setting.
                       This system setting applies only to workbooks. It doesn't apply to analyses, dashboards, or
                       pixel-perfect reports.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Workbooks use the time zone specified in the Default User Time Zone system

                             setting.
                       ｷ Off -- No time zone is applied to workbooks. Default

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After user logs out and logs in.

                       ｷ true
                       ｷ false

                       ｷ Examples for System Setting REST APIs

Data Offset for Analyses and Dashboards

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify a time zone offset of the original data that
                       users see in analyses and dashboards.

                       This system setting applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data
                       visualizations, workbooks, or pixel-perfect reports.

                       Enter an offset value that indicates the number of hours away from Greenwich Mean Time
                       (GMT) time.

                       For example, to display values in United States Eastern Standard Time (EST), which is
                       Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) - 5 hours, enter the value GMT-05:00 or the equivalent value in
                       minutes -300.

                       If you leave this setting empty, Oracle Analytics interprets this as "unknown" and performs no
                       time zone conversion.

                       If you enter 0, Oracle Analytics uses GMT to display data. If you enter 0 and set the Default
                       User Time Zone system setting, Oracle Analytics uses the default user time zone to display
                       data.

                       If you set the Default User Time Zone system setting and the user overrides it in the My
                       Account Time Zone field, Oracle Analytics uses the user's specified time zone to display data.

                       Specifying a different offset value for each user

                       If you want to specify a different offset value where session variables can be used (for
                       example, expressions, calculations), don't use the Default Data Offset Time Zone setting.
                       Instead, set the system session variable DATA_TZ in the semantic model. See About Session
                       Variables.

                       Valid Values

                       Valid offset string.

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                     April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Page 72 of 99
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.

Default User Time Zone

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify a default time zone for all users.
                       Users can specify a time zone in the My Account dialog to override this setting. If the Enable
                       Time Zone (Preview) system setting is enabled, users can specify a time zone in the My
                       Profile dialog to override this system setting.

                              Note
                                 Enable the Apply Default User Time Zone for Workbooks system setting to apply
                                 the specified time zone in workbooks.

                       The API Key and Values section below provides the REST API key name and allowed values
                       for the system setting. You must use the exact strings listed in REST operations, for example
                       (GMT-05:00) Eastern Time (US & Canada).

                       Specifying a different time zone for each user
                       If you want to specify a different offset value where session variables can be used (for
                       example, expressions, calculations), don't use the Default User Time Zone setting. Instead,
                       set the system session variable TIMEZONE in the semantic model. See About Session
                       Variables.
                       This system setting applies to workbooks, data visualizations, analyses, dashboards, and
                       pixel-perfect reports.

Time Zone for Analyses, Dashboard, Workbooks

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify the time zone used to display data in
                       analyses, dashboards, and workbooks.
                       This system setting also determines the time zone used for evaluating date calculations such
                       as getting the current date/time and timestamp values, truncating timestamp values to a date,
                       and extracting time fields from timestamp expressions in analyses, dashboards, and
                       workbooks.

                              Note
                                 To specify the time zone for a dataset's source data, open the dataset's Inspect
                                 dialog, and in the General tab go to the Time Zone field and select a time zone.

The API Key and Values section below provides the REST API key name and allowed values
for the system setting. You must use the exact strings listed in REST operations, for example
(GMT-05:00) Eastern Time (US & Canada).

This system setting applies to analyses, dashboards, and workbooks. It doesn't apply to pixel-
perfect reports.

Valid Values

ｷ (GMT) Casablanca, Monrovia

Usage Tracking System Settings

                       Administrators use these options to specify how to to monitor system usage. For example, you
                       can set the number of rows that you want stored in the usage tracking tables.

                       System Settings:
                       ｷ Enable Usage Tracking
                       ｷ Usage Tracking Connection Pool
                       ｷ Usage Tracking Init Block Table
                       ｷ Usage Tracking Logical Query Logging Table
                       ｷ Usage Tracking Maximum Rows
                       ｷ Usage Tracking Physical Query Logging Table
                       ｷ User Names as the User Identifier in Service Logs

Enable Usage Tracking

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether usage tracking is enabled or
                       disabled. When you enable this system setting, you enable all other settings in the Usage
                       Tracking section.
                       This system setting applies to the deployed semantic model. It applies to semantic models
                       created, maintained, and deployed with Semantic Modeler or Model Administration Tool.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Any enabled settings in the Usage Tracking section of this page are activated.

                             Default

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                     April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Page 83 of 99
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                            Chapter 8
                                                                                                                   Usage Tracking System Settings

                       ｷ Off -- No settings in the Usage Tracking section of this page are activated, even if they are
                             enabled.

                       Apply Required

User Names as the User Identifier in Service Logs

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether to identify users by their user name in
                       audit and diagnostic service logs. When this setting is disabled (off), users are identified by
                       their user GUID in service logs.
                       User names are logged if this setting is enabled (on), and this might make user identification
                       easier for administrators who monitor service logs through Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
                       Console. See About Audit and Diagnostic Logs for Oracle Analytics Cloud.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ On -- Record the names of users performing actions in service logs.

                             For example: "userId":"john.smith@mycompany.com"
                       ｷ Off -- Record the GUIDs of users performing actions in service logs. Default

                             For example: "userId":"aa11bb22cc33dd44ee55ff66gg77hh88"

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective
                       After a few minutes.

                       Edition
                       Professional and Enterprise.

                       API Key and Values
                       API Key -- UserNamesInServiceLogs

                       API Values --
                       ｷ true
                       ｷ false

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether workbook editors can add dataset
                       enrichments to a visualization directly from the Data Panel. This setting enables enrichments in
                       workbooks for all users.

                       knowledge enrichments for that dataset using the Enable Knowledge Enrichments option.
                       This setting applies only to workbooks and data visualizations. It doesn't apply to analyses and
                       dashboards.

                       Valid Values

                             visualization canvases. Default
                       ｷ Off -- Knowledge enrichments are not available for datasets.

Prompt Auto Complete Matching Level

                       Administrators use this system setting to specify whether the auto-complete functionality uses
                       matching to find the prompt value that the user enters into the prompt field. This setting doesn't
                       apply if the user accesses the Search dialog to locate and specify a prompt value.
                       This setting applies only to analyses and dashboards. It doesn't apply to data visualizations.

                       Valid Values
                       ｷ StartsWith -- Searches for a match that begins with the text that the user types. For

                             example, the user types M and the following stored values are displayed: MicroPod and
                             MP3 Speakers System.
                       ｷ WordStartsWith -- Searches for a match at the beginning of a word or group of words.
                             For example, the user types C and the following values are displayed: ComCell, MPEG
                             Camcorder, and 7 Megapixel Digital Camera.
                       ｷ MatchAll -- Searches for any match within the word or words. Default

                       Apply Required
                       No.

                       Change Effective

A

Frequently Asked Questions

This reference provides answers to common questions asked by administrators responsible for
configuring and managing Oracle Analytics Cloud.

Topics:

ｷ Top FAQs to Configure and Manage Oracle Analytics Cloud
     ｭ Can I see how many users are currently signed in?
     ｭ Where can I find the public key for my service?
     ｭ Is there a storage limit for datasets?
     ｭ Is there a size limit for custom knowledge files?
     ｭ Can I see the SQL generated by an analysis and analyze the log?
     ｭ What happens to my content if I terminate my subscription to Oracle Analytics Cloud?
     ｭ Can I configure a private mail server to deliver reports and visualizations from Oracle
          Analytics Cloud?
     ｭ I want to connect Oracle Analytics Cloud to a private data source over a private access
          channel. How do I do this?
     ｭ I'm having issues using TLS to connect to a data source or SMTP server. What can I
          do?

ｷ Top FAQs to Back Up and Restore User Content (Snapshots)
     ｭ What do I need to back up?
     ｭ How often should I take snapshots?
     ｭ When should I export snapshots?
     ｭ Can I use APIs to automate snapshot operations?
     ｭ Can Oracle help to restore lost content?

ｷ Top FAQs for Disaster Recovery
     ｭ What capabilities in Oracle Analytics Cloud can I use to implement a disaster recovery
          plan?
     ｭ Where can I find information about disaster recovery?

ｷ Top FAQs to Index Content and Data
     ｭ What can I index?
     ｭ What is a certified dataset?
     ｭ How often should I schedule a crawl?
     ｭ Can I index content in languages other than English?
     ｭ Are there considerations when indexing subject areas with large tables?
     ｭ How are search results ordered?
     ｭ Should I use Don't Index to secure my catalog items?

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                             April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Appendix A-1 of A-8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Appendix A
                                                                                     Top FAQs to Configure and Manage Oracle Analytics Cloud

                             ｭ How do I build an index most effectively?
                             ｭ Why are there many select distinct queries on the database during indexing?
                       ｷ Top FAQs to Configure and Manage Publisher
                             ｭ How do I configure a delivery channel for Publisher?
                             ｭ How do I restrict access to delivery channels?
                             ｭ How do I configure FTP and SFTP delivery retry?
                             ｭ How can I enable the viewing of audit data in Publisher?
                             ｭ How do I upload the configuration-specific files?

Top FAQs to Configure and Manage Oracle Analytics Cloud

                       The top FAQs for configuring and managing Oracle Analytics Cloud are identified in this topic.

                       Can I see how many users are currently signed in?

                       Yes. Display the home page , click Console, and then click Sessions and Query Logs. See
                       Monitoring Users Who Are Signed In.

                       Where can I find the public key for my service?

                       Display the home page, click Console, Database Connections, click the menu icon, and then
                       click Get Public Key.

                       Is there a storage limit for datasets?

                       Oracle Analytics Cloud has a fixed 250 GB storage quota for data files that's shared across all
                       users. The limit for an individual user is 50 GB. When users leave the organization,
                       administrators can delete their unused datasets to free up storage space.

                       Is there a size limit for shared images?

                       Yes. The size limit for an individual shared image is 10MB. Additionally, the overall limit for all
                       shared images in cloud storage is 50GB.

                       Is there a size limit for custom knowledge files?

                       Yes. The maximum file size you can upload is 250 MB.

                       I'm having issues using TLS to connect to a data source or SMTP server. What can I do?

                       Starting in the May 2025 update, data source connections that use TLS now use enhanced
                       encryption and security configuration. You might experience issues if a database or SMTP
                       server that you connect to using TLS doesn't support TLSv1.2. Here are some issues you
                       might encounter:
                       ｷ Existing reports and dashboards fail and display a failure message if Oracle Analytics can't

                             connect to a data source using TLS.
                       ｷ Errors display when you try to create a connection to a database or SMTP server using

                             TLS.

                       Sign in to My Oracle Support (https://support.oracle.com) and read the article TLS Security
                       Updates for Connections in Oracle Analytics (KB127925). If you're still unable to resolve your
                       issue, log a Service Request with Oracle Support.

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                             April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Appendix A-2 of A-8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                                           Appendix A
                                                                                   Top FAQs to Back Up and Restore User Content (Snapshots)

                       Can I see the SQL generated by an analysis and analyze the log?

                       Yes. Display the home page, click Console, and then click Sessions and Query Logs. See
                       Analyzing SQL Queries and Logs.

                       What happens to my content if I terminate my subscription to Oracle Analytics Cloud?

                       Before you terminate your subscription, take a snapshot of your system, that is, the latest
                       semantic model, catalog content, application roles, and so on. If you subscribe to Oracle
                       Analytics Cloud in the future, you can import content from this archive file.

                       See Uploading Snapshots and Restoring from a Snapshot.

                       Can I change the default logo and dashboard style for the whole deployment?

                       Yes. When logged in as Administrator, navigate to the Classic Home page, click the user profile
                       icon, click Administration, and then click Manage Themes. Create a new theme including
                       dashboard properties such as logo, branding, page colors, and link colors, and click Active.
                       This new style is applied for all new browser sessions.

                       Can I upload a semantic model RPD file from Oracle BI Enterprise Edition and Oracle
                       Analytics Server?

                       Yes. If you've modeled your business data with Oracle BI Enterprise Edition or Oracle Analytics
                       Server, then you don't need to start from scratch in Oracle Analytics Cloud.

                       ｷ Semantic Modeler - You can upload your RPD file to Semantic Modeler. See Import a File
                             to Create a Semantic Model.

                       Can I configure a private mail server to deliver reports and visualizations from Oracle
                       Analytics Cloud?

                       For secure access to email delivery, Oracle recommends you use Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
                       (OCI) Email Delivery. OCI Email Delivery provides a fully-managed, secure solution within OCI
                       with a rich set of governance and observability features. See Use the SMTP Mail Server in
                       Oracle Cloud Infrastructure for Email Delivery.

                       In addition, Oracle Analytics Cloud supports SMTP mail servers that are accessible from the
                       public internet. See Set Up an Email Server to Deliver Reports. If your publicly accessible
                       SMTP mail server uses an allowlist to restrict access, find the Gateway IP Address of your
                       OAC instance and add it to your mail server's allowlist. See Find the Gateway IP Address of
                       your OAC Instance.

                       I want to connect Oracle Analytics Cloud to a private data source over a private access
                       channel. How do I do this?

                       You use Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Console to set up a private access channel for Oracle
                       Analytics Cloud and configure access to your private data sources. See Connect to Private
                       Data Sources Through a Private Access Channel and Top FAQs for Private Data Sources in
                       Administering Oracle Analytics Cloud on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (Gen 2).

Top FAQs to Back Up and Restore User Content (Snapshots)

                       The top FAQs for backing up and restoring user content are identified in this topic.

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                             April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Appendix A-3 of A-8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                Appendix A
                                                            Top FAQs to Back Up and Restore User Content (Snapshots)

What do I need to back up?

Oracle recommends that you regularly back up all the content that users create to a file called
a snapshot. User content includes catalog content such as reports, dashboards, data
visualization workbooks, pixel perfect reports, datasets, data flows, semantic models, security
roles, service settings, and so on.

If something goes wrong with your content or service, you can revert to the content you saved
in a snapshot. Snapshots are also useful if you want to move or share content from one
service to another.

To back up user content, see Take a Snapshot.

To restore user content, see Restore from a Snapshot.

How often should I take snapshots?

Oracle recommends that you take snapshots at significant checkpoints, for example, before
you make a major change to your content or environment. In addition, Oracle recommends that
you take regular weekly snapshots or at your own defined frequency based on the rate of
change of your environment and rollback requirements.

You can keep up to 40 snapshots online and export as many as you want offline (that is, to
your local file system or to your own Oracle Cloud storage).

When should I export snapshots?

Oracle recommends that you adopt a regular practice of exporting snapshots to offline storage.
You can export snapshots to your own file system and store them locally. Or, you can export
snapshots to your own Oracle Cloud storage. See Export Snapshots.

If you regularly export large snapshots (over 5GB or larger than the download limit of your
browser), Oracle recommends that you set up a storage bucket on Oracle Cloud and save your
snapshots to cloud storage. This way, you can avoid export errors due to size limitations and
timeouts that can sometimes occur when you export snapshots on your local file system. See
Set Up a Oracle Cloud Storage Bucket for Snapshots.

Can I use APIs to automate snapshot operations?

Yes. See Manage Snapshots Using REST APIs.

Can Oracle help to restore lost content?

No. Customer data backup, retention, and recovery or restoration is the sole responsibility of
the customer using snapshots (BAR files), catalog archives (CATALOG files), and export
archives (DVA files). Oracle-managed infrastructure backups are created to maintain the
service in the event of an infrastructure incident. Oracle-maintained backups aren't provided for
user-created data management. See Oracle PaaS and IaaS Public Cloud Services - Pillar
document.

Oracle recommends that you use the Logging service in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure to track
and troubleshoot content changes between snapshots. When you enable usage and diagnostic
logs, you can monitor create, update, delete, and permission change operations on all catalog
objects, such as classic analyses, dashboards, workbooks, pixel-perfect reports, folders,
datasets, self-service connections, data flows, sequences, scripts, and so on. See Monitor
Usage and Diagnostic Logs.

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                             April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Appendix A-4 of A-8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                 Appendix A
                                                       Top FAQs for Disaster Recovery

                       Can I use a snapshot to migrate from test to production

                       Yes, you can use snapshots to migrate Oracle Analytics Cloud content and configuration from
                       test to production environments. Snapshots capture the state of your test environment at a
                       specific point in time, allowing you to restore that state in your production environment (with or
                       without user data, user folder content, or connection credentials).

                       ｷ Migrate everything - Use this option when the users and data sources are the same in both
                             environments.

                       ｷ Migrate without user folder content - Use this option as part of user acceptance testing,
                             where test users have different content but access to the same data sources.

                       ｷ Migrate without connection credentials - Use this option if all you need to do is to move
                             content and configuration from one environment to another after it's been tested (no users
                             in common, and different data source security).

                       Customers are responsible for keeping test and production environments in sync. Any content
                       that you create directly in the production environment will be replaced or lost.

Top FAQs for Disaster Recovery

                       The top FAQs for disaster recovery are identified in this topic.

                       What capabilities in Oracle Analytics Cloud can I use to implement a disaster recovery
                       plan?

                       Oracle Analytics Cloud offers several features that you can implement to minimize disruption
                       for users:

                       ｷ Snapshots: Oracle recommends that you back up user content regularly to a snapshot. If
                             required, you can restore the content in your snapshot to a redundant Oracle Analytics
                             Cloud environment. See Take Snapshots and Restore.

                       ｷ Pause and resume: You can deploy a passive backup Oracle Analytics Cloud
                             environment, and use the pause and resume feature to control metering and minimize
                             costs. See Pause and Resume a Service.

                       ｷ Diverse regional availability: Oracle Analytics Cloud is available in several global
                             regions. You can deploy a redundant Oracle Analytics Cloud environment in a different
                             region to mitigate the risk of region-wide events. See Data Regions for Platform and
                             Infrastructure Services.

                       Where can I find information about disaster recovery?

                       See Technical Papers. For additional help or assistance, engage consulting resources (Oracle
                       or a third party) or reach out to Oracle Analytics Community.

Top FAQs to Index Content and Data

                       The top FAQs for indexing semantic models and catalog content are identified in this topic.

                       What can I index?

                       Administrators can choose to index:

                       ｷ Semantic models - Subject area, dimensions names and values, and measure names and
                             values. You must be an administrator to modify the semantic model indexing preferences.

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                             April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Appendix A-5 of A-8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                                                Appendix A
                                                                                      Top FAQs to Index Content and Data

ｷ Catalog content - Workbooks, analyses, dashboards, and reports. You must be an
     administrator to modify the catalog indexing preferences.

ｷ File-based datasets - You can index a file-based dataset so that specified users can build
     visualizations with a dataset's data. Or you can certify a file-based dataset so that the
     specified users can search for its data from the home page. Any user can set a file-based
     dataset to index or certify the dataset.

See Configure Data Model Search Indexing.

What is a certified dataset?

Any user can upload a spreadsheet to create a dataset, and uploaded spreadsheets can be of
varying quality. When a user certifies a shared dataset, it means that the user is confirming that
the dataset contains good, reliable data that other users can search for from the home page.
When you and users who've been granted access to datasets search from the home page, the
data in a certified dataset is ranked high in the search results.

How often should I schedule a crawl?

The index updates automatically as users add or modify catalog content. By default, the
catalog and semantic model crawl run once per month. In some cases you might want to
change this default after importing a BAR file, if automatic indexing didn't run, or if user activity
patterns clearly require it. Frequent crawling uses significant resources, so avoid increasing the
crawl schedule frequency more than necessary.

Can I index content in languages other than English?

Yes. You can index content in 28 languages.

ｷ Semantic models and catalog content - You can generate indexes for multiple
     languages at the same time. Go to the Search Index page and Ctl-click to select one or
     more of the 28 available languages. For example, if your company's headquarters are in
     the United States and you have offices in Italy, you might select English and italiano to
     create indexes in both English and Italian. See Configure Data Model Search Indexing.

ｷ Datasets - You can index a dataset for a single language at a time. Go to the Inspect
     dialog for the dataset and select one of the 28 available languages. See Index a Dataset.

        Note

         If your data is in English and your index language is English, you can't search for the
         data in a different language such as French. For example, if your data includes
         English product names (such as chair, desk, matches), you can't search using French
         product names (such as chaise, bureau, alumettes).

Are there considerations when indexing subject areas with large tables?

You can index any size table, but big tables take longer to index. For large subject areas that
have many tables or large tables, consider indexing only the columns your users need to
search for.

Because index files are compact, it's rare to exceed the storage space that Oracle Analytics
reserves for indexing.

How are search results ordered?

Search results are listed in this order:

Configuring Oracle Analytics Cloud                             April 15, 2026
G48416-07                                              Appendix A-6 of A-8
Copyright ｩ 2017, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates.
                                                                                                  Appendix A
                                                       Top FAQs to Configure and Manage Publisher

                       1. Semantic model (semantic layer)
                       2. Certified datasets
                       3. Personal datasets
                       4. Catalog items (workbooks, analyses, dashboards, and reports)

                       Should I use Don't Index to secure my catalog items?

                       No. Oracle doesn't recommend setting the Crawl Status field to Don't Index as a way of
                       hiding a catalog item from users. Users won't see the item in search results or on the home
                       page, but are still able to access the item. Instead, use permissions to apply the proper
                       security to the item.

                       How do I build an index most effectively?

                       For best results only index the subject areas, dimensions, catalog items, and certify datasets
                       that users need to find. Indexing all items yields too many search results. Oracle recommends
                       that you deselect all semantic model and catalog items and then select only the items that the
                       user needs. You can then add items to the index as needed. For tips on indexing catalog
                       content, see About Search Indexing.

                       Why are there many select distinct queries on the database during indexing?

                       This is most likely because the semantic model's indexing option is set to Index. When you set
                       this option to Index, the metadata and values are indexed, which means that during indexing
                       the select distinct queries are run to fetch the data values for all of the columns in all of the
                       subject areas that are configured for indexing.

                       If this system overhead isn't acceptable or if users don't need the additional functionality to
                       visualize data values from the search bar on the home page, then go to the Console, click
                       Search Index, and set the indexing option to Index Metadata Only. Setting this option to
                       Index Metadata Only indexes dimension and measure names, only, and doesn't run select
                       distinct queries.

Top FAQs to Configure and Manage Publisher

                       The top FAQs for configuring and managing Publisher are identified in this topic.

                       How do I configure a delivery channel for Publisher?

                       Use the Publisher administration page to add a connection to a delivery channel and test the
                       connection.

                       How do I restrict access to delivery channels?

                       You can configure role-based access for delivery channels. In the delivery channel
                       configuration page, from the Available Roles list, select one or more roles you want to provide
                       access to the delivery channel, and add them to the Allowed Roles list.

                       How do I configure FTP and SFTP delivery retry?

                       If you set the Enable FTP/SFTP delivery retry runtime property to true, Publisher makes
                       another attempt to deliver reports to the FTP or SFTP delivery channel, if the first attempt fails.

                       How can I enable the viewing of audit data in Publisher?

                       Use the Enable Monitor and Audit property in the Publisher Server Configuration page to
                       enable or disable viewing of the audit data of Publisher catalog objects.

How do I upload the configuration-specific files?
Use Upload Center in the Publisher system administration page to upload and manage
configurationｭspecific files for font, digital signature, ICC profile, SSH private key, SSL
certificate, and JDBC client certificate.

What is the size limit for emails?
15MB is the maximum size of an e-mail message that Oracle.com will accept from the Internet
or deliver from Oracle.com. That means the sum of the sizes of message text, headers,
attachments, and any embedded images must be less than 15MB.
